que: What is mutable state in Compose?
options: Data that can change and update UI

que: Which API creates observable state in Compose?
Why does Compose use state-driven UI?
options: So UI reacts to data changes declaratively
Moving state to a higher-level composable
Using normal variables for changing UI data

que: Which API can help preserve simple UI state across recreation?
What usually happens when mutable state changes?
Why keep business logic out of low-level composables?
options: To keep UI simpler and more reusable

que: What kind of models are often preferred for UI data flow?
options: Mostly immutable models with controlled updates

que: When do ordinary variables fail for reactive UI?
options: When Compose cannot observe their changes

que: Which statement best describes Compose state?
options: UI reads state, and state changes drive recomposition
UI must be manually invalidated like old Views everywhere
